title: Aphragmus hinkuensis
intro: Aphragmus hinkuensis, also known as Himalayan meadow cress, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its delicate white flowers and edible leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Aphragmus hinkuensis include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aphragmus hinkuensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be consumed raw as a salad green, while its roots are sometimes used to make a tincture or dried extract.
taxonomy: Aphragmus hinkuensis is classified within the plant family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Aphragmus and is a type of flowering plant. The species is native to the Himalayan region and is characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow leaves. Aphragmus hinkuensis is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Brassicaceae family. Further classification of the species at the rank of subspecies or variety may not be well-defined.
